Transformation · S120 Module

Our Mission

Closing the loop on
industrial carbon

MARYSEVE's patented S120 Module intercepts industrial CO₂ before atmospheric release and transforms it into Mineral Sap — a suite of high-purity mineral compounds for local agriculture and industry.

Founded in Ouagadougou by the General Manager, MARYSEVE is the only African company with a patented, production-ready CO₂ mineralization system. Our model creates sovereign supply of critical minerals while eliminating import dependency and generating carbon credits.

Mineral Sap™ — MARYSEVE's Branded Mineral Output
Three-Stage Process

Capture. Transform. Valorize.

01 Capture

Industrial CO₂ streams from cement plants, mines, and energy facilities are intercepted at source by the S120 Module before atmospheric release.

02 Mineral Transformation

OAPI & ISO certified reactions convert CO₂ into stable Mineral Sap — carbonates, bicarbonates, and oxides with defined commercial purity grades.

03 Valorization

Certified Mineral Sap reaches Burkina Faso's agriculture, construction, and industry across 11 departments — replacing imports and generating carbon credits simultaneously.

Phase I · Ouagadougou Industrial Zone · Burkina Faso

The MARYSEVE facility — built in Burkina Faso, for Burkina Faso

Our Origin

Rooted in West Africa.
Reaching the world.

MARYSEVE is headquartered in Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso — positioned at the geographic and economic heart of West Africa. Our OAPI patent protects operations across 17 nations, and our distribution network spans 11 of Burkina Faso's 13 departments.

Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso — HQ
17 OAPI Nations — Patent Coverage
11 Departments — Distribution

Carbon is not a waste product. It is a feedstock waiting for the right process — the S120 Module.

ERWAN · Founder, MARYSEVE

The S120 Module

A patented pathway from exhaust to Mineral Sap

The S120 Module is MARYSEVE's production-ready CO₂ valorization system — designed and patented in Burkina Faso, certified under OAPI and ISO standards, and engineered for tropical industrial environments.

OAPI Patent · Active
ISO Certified

S120 Module · In Operation

Industrial CO₂ capture & Mineral Sap synthesis
at the point of emission

OAPI Patent
ISO Certified
01
Stage One

CO₂ Capture & Concentration

Industrial flue gases enter the S120 Module's absorption unit. Proprietary alkaline solvents selectively bind CO₂ molecules. The solvent regenerates under controlled heat, releasing concentrated CO₂ at >95% purity — ready for mineral synthesis.

S120 · Unit A
02
Stage Two

Mineral Sap™ Synthesis Reactor

Concentrated CO₂ meets locally sourced alkaline feedstocks in the synthesis reactor. Controlled temperature, pressure, and pH direct crystallization — producing targeted Mineral Sap compounds with defined granulometry and purity grades certified to ISO standards.

S120 · Unit B
03
Stage Three

Drying, Milling & QC Certification

Mineral Sap precipitates are filtered, spray-dried, and milled to application-specific granulometries. Each batch undergoes XRF and titration analysis. Products are packaged in 25 kg and 1-tonne format with full ISO certification documentation.

S120 · Unit C

Capacity

Industrial scale, local impact

The S120 facility is engineered to scale — from Phase I pilot to 2 billion litres of certified Mineral Sap per year.

2B
Litres of Mineral Sap produced per facility per year at full capacity
95%+
CO₂ purity achieved in the capture stage before mineral synthesis
11
Burkina Faso departments reached by Mineral Sap distribution network
97%
Carbon permanently fixed in mineral form — no re-emission risk
S120 Module · Phase Objectives

Targets that define each deployment phase

2B

Litres / Year

Target annual Mineral Sap output at Phase III full-scale facility. Equivalent to displacing 2B litres of imported mineral input across West Africa.

95%+

CO₂ Capture Purity

Minimum purity target for the CO₂ stream post-absorption unit — enabling consistent crystallization grade in the synthesis reactor.

97%

Permanent Carbon Fix

Percentage of captured CO₂ locked permanently in mineral form (CaCO₃, NaHCO₃, MgCO₃). No re-emission — fully eligible for CDR carbon credits.

98%+

Mineral Sap Purity

ISO-certified commercial purity grade for CaCO₃ output — matching or exceeding imported industrial specifications at 15–25% lower cost.

500t

CO₂ / Year · Phase I Pilot

5,000t

CO₂ / Year · Phase II Scale

50,000t

CO₂ / Year · Phase III Regional

17

OAPI Nations · License Target

Intellectual Property

Protected under OAPI & ISO

The S120 Module is registered with the Organisation Africaine de la Propriété Intellectuelle (OAPI) — granting patent protection across 17 member states covering the majority of West and Central Africa. ISO certification validates manufacturing and quality control standards.

Our IP covers the solvent formulation, reactor configuration, and crystallization control methodology. This makes MARYSEVE the only African company with a legally protected, production-certified CO₂ mineralization process.

S120 Module · On-Site

Ouagadougou Industrial Zone
Burkina Faso

Mineral Sap™ Applications

One process. Many minerals.

The S120 Module's synthesis reactor is configurable — adjusting reaction parameters produces different Mineral Sap compounds for different end markets. Each product is ISO-certified and tested before delivery across 11 departments.

Calcium Carbonate (CaCO₃)

Ground calcium carbonate for cement production, paint, paper filler, and plastic compounding. 98%+ purity — replaces imported material from Europe and the Maghreb at 15–25% below import price.

Construction · Manufacturing · Plastics

Sodium Bicarbonate (NaHCO₃)

Food-grade and technical baking soda. Used in bread production, water treatment, fire suppression, and pharmaceutical manufacturing across the ECOWAS region.

Food Processing · Water Treatment · Pharma

Magnesium Carbonate (MgCO₃)

Light magnesium carbonate for fertilizer blending, refractory ceramics, and rubber manufacturing. Addresses a critical gap in West Africa's agricultural input supply chain.

Agriculture · Ceramics · Rubber

Agricultural Lime

Soil amendment for Burkina Faso's laterite-dominated farmlands. Corrects acidity, increases crop yields, and sequesters carbon in the soil — supporting both food security and climate goals.

Agriculture · Soil Health · Carbon Sequestration

Construction Aggregates

Calcite-based mineral filler and aggregate for lightweight concrete blocks and mortar. Engineered to Burkina Faso construction standards as a low-carbon alternative to conventional quarried materials.

Construction · Infrastructure · Housing

Carbon Credits (Pre-Order)

Each tonne of CO₂ permanently mineralized generates a verified Carbon Removal Credit (CDR) eligible for Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. Pre-orders open for qualifying buyers and climate investors.

Carbon Markets · Climate Finance · ESG

Pre-Order Available

Every litre of Mineral Sap produced is an import replaced, a carbon credit earned, and a farmer or builder served locally.

MARYSEVE · Product Philosophy

The MARYSEVE Model

Sovereignty, Excellence, Dignity.

MARYSEVE operates at the intersection of industrial decarbonization, local mineral production, and African sovereignty. Our triple-stream revenue model is built to be commercially self-sustaining while delivering measurable social and environmental impact.

Triple Revenue Stream
CDR Carbon Credits

Each tonne permanently mineralized generates a verified Carbon Removal Credit (CDR) eligible for Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. Burkina Faso is an active participant. Pre-orders are open for climate buyers.

M₁ Mineral Sap™ Sales

Certified Mineral Sap is sold directly to industrial buyers, construction firms, and agricultural distributors across 11 departments. Priced 15–25% below imported equivalents — providing immediate buyer savings while maintaining healthy margins.

Sᵥ Industrial Service Contracts

Mining operations, cement plants, and energy companies partner with MARYSEVE on long-term CO₂ capture service agreements. The client gains documented emission reduction; MARYSEVE receives stable feedstock and a per-tonne service fee.

Economic Value Proposition

Better for buyers. Better for the continent.

Burkina Faso imports nearly all industrial minerals from Europe, Asia, and North Africa — adding transportation carbon, currency risk, and supply chain fragility. MARYSEVE's local Mineral Sap eliminates all three permanently.

Our structural cost advantage is permanent: our feedstock — CO₂ — costs nothing except the S120 Module chemistry to transform it. That asymmetry scales with production capacity and is protected by OAPI patent from competition.

Skilled positions within MARYSEVE pay an average salary of 1 180 000 FCFA — creating anchor employment in the local industrial economy while training Burkina Faso's next generation of process chemists and engineers.

Loi d'ingénierie · Engineering Principle

Gamme VET : produit 100% pur — ne jamais administrer sans dilution dans l'eau de boisson. Gamme AGRO : séparation stricte des matrices Sol (acide d'attaque pur) et Foliaire (dilution contrôlée). Statut : Stérile (0 coliformes, 0 As/Pb) · pH 5,15 · Certifié ISO.

About MARYSEVE

Born in Burkina Faso.
Built for Africa.

MARYSEVE was founded on a simple conviction: that Africa's industrial challenges are best solved by African innovation, protected by African intellectual property, and commercialized for African markets first.

E

Erwan Beneteau de La Prairie

Founder & Director General

"We are not building a company. We are building Burkina Faso's first sovereign mineral supply chain — with a CO₂ feedstock that costs nothing except the will to use it."

contact@maryseve.com
Vision & Leadership

Industrial sovereignty from Ouagadougou

Erwan Beneteau de La Prairie founded MARYSEVE after identifying a structural gap in West Africa's industrial economy: the continent emits billions of tonnes of CO₂ while simultaneously importing the mineral compounds that could be derived from that same CO₂.

The solution was the S120 Module — a compact, scalable CO₂ mineralization unit engineered for tropical industrial conditions and patented through OAPI. From Ouagadougou, MARYSEVE now has the infrastructure to produce Mineral Sap at 2 billion litres per year.

MARYSEVE's governance model ensures that value created through Mineral Sap production and carbon credits stays within Burkina Faso's economy — building institutional capacity alongside industrial capacity.

Leadership & Governance

the General Manager

A company that regenerates the earth must first regenerate the people who work within it.

Our Commitments

Excellence in every dimension

IP Intellectual Sovereignty

Every innovation, process, and product originates in Burkina Faso and is protected by African IP law. Our OAPI patent is the foundation of our competitive moat across 17 nations.

ISO Quality Certification

Mineral Sap is manufactured and tested to ISO standards — making it acceptable to international buyers, eligible for compliance markets, and comparable to imported alternatives on every technical specification.

BF Local First

92% local employment. 78% local feedstock sourcing. 88% of revenue retained in-country. MARYSEVE's operations are deliberately structured to maximize value capture within Burkina Faso's economy.

Partners & Investors

Built on strategic alliances

MARYSEVE seeks partners who understand that the most durable industrial solutions are those built within Africa, by Africans, and protected by African law. We operate at the nexus of climate finance, industrial chemistry, and sovereign development.

Strategic Partners
OAPI Organisation Africaine de la Propriété Intellectuelle

Patent registrar providing legal protection for the S120 Module across 17 African states.

IP · Legal Framework
Investment Opportunity

We are raising our Series Seed

MARYSEVE is seeking strategic investment to fund Phase I pilot construction and commercial validation of the S120 Module. We target climate-focused impact funds, African development finance institutions, and industrial strategic investors.

Eligible investors receive a combination of financial returns, verified carbon credit portfolios, and preferential Mineral Sap supply agreements as the business scales. A full investment memorandum is available under NDA.

Investment Objectives

I

OAPI + ISO protected S120 Module — defensible across 17 African markets

II

Triple revenue: Mineral Sap sales + carbon credits + industrial service fees

III

2B litres/year capacity at full scale — 11 departments served with local Mineral Sap

IV

First-mover in permanent carbon removal on a continent importing $2B+ in minerals annually
